info: In German, the present tense (Präsens) is used to describe actions that are happening now, general truths, habitual actions, or actions in the near future. The conjugation of verbs in the present tense depends on the subject (ich, du, er/sie/es, wir, ihr, sie/Sie).

info: Bedeuten is used to express meaning or definition (signs, words, symbols).
For “give blood,” German normally uses spenden (“to donate”), not geben.
native_answer: we give blood once a year
answer: Wir spenden einmal im Jahr Blut.
audio_dir: Wir spenden einmal im Jahr Blut.

info: Geben is used with fixed expressions like sein Bestes geben (“to give one’s best”).
native_answer: I always give my best effort

info: Geben is used for giving money or tangible things directly.
native_answer: I give money when I can
answer: Ich gebe Geld, wenn ich kann.
audio_dir: Ich gebe Geld, wenn ich kann.

info: German uses simple present; no continuous tense
Simple present used
Simple present used
Simple present used
Simple present used

Simple present used

info: Simple present used
native_answer: You are helping him get up
